title: synth_xilinx is discarding tristate logic
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue
```verilog
module top(inout pin, input dout, sel, output din);
assign pin = sel ? dout : 1'bz;
assign din = pin;
endmodule
```
`yosys -p "synth_xilinx; dump" tristate.v`
## Expected behavior

Yosys either instantiates a tristate buffer that downstream tools can understand; or at least prints a clearer warning/error that the tristate is being totally discarded for `synth_xilinx`, rather than the ambiguous `Yosys has only limited support for tri-state logic at the moment.` warning.

## Actual behavior

```
attribute \top 1
attribute \src "tristate.v:1"
module \top

  attribute \src "tristate.v:1"
  wire output 4 \din

  attribute \src "tristate.v:1"
  wire input 2 \dout

  attribute \src "tristate.v:1"
  wire inout 1 \pin

  attribute \src "tristate.v:1"
  wire input 3 \sel

  connect \din \dout
  connect \pin \dout
end
```
code_blocks:
attribute \top 1
attribute \src "tristate.v:1"
module \top

  attribute \src "tristate.v:1"
  wire output 4 \din

  attribute \src "tristate.v:1"
  wire input 2 \dout

  attribute \src "tristate.v:1"
  wire inout 1 \pin

  attribute \src "tristate.v:1"
  wire input 3 \sel

  connect \din \dout
  connect \pin \dout
end
